channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: McNeeley scores 17 points as No. 25 UConn rallies to a 76-72 win over No. 15 Baylor
item.description: STORRS, Conn. (AP) — Liam McNeeley scored 17 points and No. 25 UConn came back from an early deficit to beat No. 15 Baylor 76-72 on Wednesday night in the Big 12-Big East Battle.
item.dc:creator: JIM FULLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michigan flipped QB Bryce Underwood with some help from Oracle founder Larry Ellison and Tom Brady
item.description: BELLEVILLE, Mich. (AP) — Bryce Underwood, encircled by a slew of reporters and cameras, was relieved to make his commitment to Michigan official with his signature as college football signing day kicked off on Wednesday. “It's time for the next level,” Underwood said.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: League upholds 3-game suspension for Houston LB Azeez Al-Shaair following hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: The NFL and the NFL players association are upholding Houston linebacker Azeez Al-Shaair’s three-game suspension without pay for repeated violations of player safety rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Bryce Underwood, Elijah Dotson’s commitment to Michigan football marks historic recruiting flip
item.description: College football teams are adding to their Early National Signing Day rosters, including quarterback Bryce Underwood, the 2025 class’s No. 1 player and No. 2 recruit in the state in four-star athlete Elijah Dotson.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Jaguars' Doug Pederson defends team for melee after Azeez Al-Shaair's illegal hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: Jaguars coach Doug Pederson defended his team on Wednesday after Texans player Azeez Al-Shaair hit Trevor Lawrence, inciting an intense brawl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Josh Allen's creativity and Jameis Winston's risk-taking produce statistical firsts in the NFL
item.description: Inside the Numbers dives into NFL statistics, streaks and trends each week. For more Inside the Numbers, head here. ___ Josh Allen and Jameis Winston delivered signature performances in prime time that define their styles of play.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Miami's playoff hopes nosedive as Alabama rises in the latest College Football Playoff rankings
item.description: Miami's playoff hopes took an all-but-final nosedive while Alabama's got a boost Tuesday night in the last rankings before the 12-team College Football Playoff bracket is set next weekend. The Hurricanes (10-2) moved down six spots to No.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick, Marcus Freeman among favorites to be the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, oddsmakers say
item.description: Former New England Patriots head coach Bill Belichick and current Chicago Bears interim head coach Thomas Brown are among the early favorites to be the Bears' next head coach, according to sports gambling outlets.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: BYU runners use olive oil to win NCAA championship
item.description: The school's coach said his runners used “an old-school running trick” by rubbing olive oil on their legs to keep themselves warm.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Texas safety Michael Taaffe honors Jake Ehlinger, 'feels sorry' for fan who made sign mocking death
item.description: Taaffe had strong, passionate comments regarding a sign held up by a fan in the crowd for ESPN's College Gameday, broadcasting from the Texas A&M campus the morning before the big game.
item.dc:creator: Billy Gates, NewsNation

item.title: Christian McCaffrey's season 'potentially' over after knee injury in 49ers' loss to Bills
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jazz come up short against Lakers, 105-104
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Anthony Davis had 33 points and 11 rebounds, LeBron James added 27 points and 14 assists, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers beat the Utah Jazz 105-104 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Monahan with goal and assist, Blue Jackets extend points streak to 6 with 6-3 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Sean Monahan had a goal and assist, and the Columbus Blue Jackets extended their points streak to six games with a 6-3 win over the Chicago Blackhawks on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kyrie Irving scores 30 to lead Dallas past Jazz, 106-94
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Kyrie Irving went for 30 points, as the Dallas Mavericks beat the Utah Jazz for a second time this season, 106-94.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: USU wins NIT season tip-off over North Texas, 61-57
item.description: ORLANDO (ABC4 Sports) - A career-best scoring performance from Mason Falslev helped lead Utah State men’s basketball (7-0, 0-0 MW) to a championship game title at the NIT Season Tip-Off Friday evening, as the Aggies defeated North Texas 61-57 at the ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, WGN 9

item.title: Lions beat Bears 23-20 to extend win streak to 10 games
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Jared Goff threw two touchdown passes to Sam LaPorta, Jake Bates made three field goals in the first half and the Detroit Lions held off the Chicago Bears 23-20 on Thursday to extend their winning streak to 10 games. The Lions snapped a seven-game losing streak on Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago looks to break Boston's 6-game win streak
item.description: Boston Celtics (15-3, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (8-12, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Friday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Jose State is disappointed Boise State forfeited but looks forward to volleyball final
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — San Jose State said Thursday it was disappointed Boise State forfeited its semifinal match in the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament but that the Spartans were focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hall's fifth career hat trick leads Blackhawks over Stars 6-2
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Taylor Hall scored his fifth career hat trick, Connor Bedard, Teuvo Teravainen and Louis Crevier also scored, and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Dallas Stars 6-2 on Wednesday night for their second win in three games. Hall’s game-opening goal 17 seconds in set the tone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
